SEARCH
Package eu.algites.lib.common.enums.uiddata

Class AIsUidEnumDataUtils

java.lang.Object
eu.algites.lib.common.enums.uiddata.AIsUidEnumDataUtils
public class AIsUidEnumDataUtils extends Object

Title: AIsUidEnumDataUtils

Description: Utilities for the Enum uiddata handling. especially for creating and parsing globally unique Identifications of the enum uiddata from and to UIDs.

UID format with i+2 specific segments (always at least 3 or more ): <origin-class>:<namespace>:<specific-segment-1>[:<specific-segment-2[ ... [:<specific-segment-i>]]>]

  • originClass is builtin or custom
  • For builtin, namespace MUST be empty
  • For custom, namespace MUST be non-empty
  • specific-segment-i MAY be empty or non according to the given usage and enum uiddata specification
  • fileType MUST be non-empty

Copyright: Copyright (c) 2026 Artur Linhart, Algites

Company: Algites

Date:
13.01.26 17:08

  • Method Details

    • createUid

      public static <O extends AIiUidEnumDataOrigin> String createUid(@Nonnull O aOrigin, String aUidNamespacePart, @Nonnull List<String> aSpecificUidParts, @Nonnull List<AIiUidPartMetadata<O>> aSpecificUidPartsMetadata)
      Create a UID with specified parameters.

      Example: builtin:::jar or builtin::sources:jar

      Type Parameters:
      O - origin type
      Parameters:
      aOrigin - origin of the UID
      aUidNamespacePart - metadata for the namespace segment (empty for builtin)
      aSpecificUidParts - parts of the UID after LAST_UID_HEADER_PART_POSITION to the end
      aSpecificUidPartsMetadata - metadata for the specific parts
      Returns:
      UID string for the builtin UID

    • createBuiltinUid

      public static String createBuiltinUid(@Nonnull List<String> aSpecificUidParts, @Nonnull List<AIiUidPartMetadata<AInUidEnumDataOrigin>> aSpecificUidPartsMetadata)
      Create a Builtin UID with specified parameters. Uses AInUidEnumDataOrigin.BUILTIN as origin and empty namespace.

      Example: builtin:::jar or builtin::sources:jar

      Parameters:
      aSpecificUidParts - parts of the UID after LAST_UID_HEADER_PART_POSITION to the end
      aSpecificUidPartsMetadata - metadata for the specific parts
      Returns:
      UID string for the builtin UID

    • createCustomUid

      public static String createCustomUid(String aUidNamespacePart, @Nonnull List<String> aSpecificUidParts, @Nonnull List<AIiUidPartMetadata<AInUidEnumDataOrigin>> aSpecificUidPartsMetadata)
      Create a Builtin UID with specified parameters. Uses AInUidEnumDataOrigin.BUILTIN as origin.

      Example: builtin:::jar or builtin::sources:jar

      Parameters:
      aUidNamespacePart - metadata for the namespace segment (empty for builtin)
      aSpecificUidParts - parts of the UID after LAST_UID_HEADER_PART_POSITION to the end
      aSpecificUidPartsMetadata - metadata for the specific parts
      Returns:
      UID string for the builtin UID

    • parseUid

      public static <R extends AIiUidEnumDataRecord, O extends AIiUidEnumDataOrigin, GUEDT extends AIiUidEnumDataType<? extends R, O>> R parseUid(@Nonnull GUEDT aEnumDataType, @Nonnull String aUid)
      Parse and validate an OutputType UID.
      Type Parameters:
      R - type of the result enum uiddata type expected.
      O - origin type
      GUEDT - type of the globally unique enum uiddata type
      Parameters:
      aEnumDataType - enum uiddata type for which the result has to be returned
      aUid - uid string
      Returns:
      parsed parts
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if invalid

    • validateUid

      public static <R extends AIiUidEnumDataRecord, O extends AIiUidEnumDataOrigin, GUEDT extends AIiUidEnumDataType<? extends R, O>> void validateUid(@Nonnull GUEDT aEnumDataType, @Nonnull String aUid) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Validate a UID (throws on error).
      Type Parameters:
      R - type of the enum uiddata type expected.
      O - origin type
      Parameters:
      aEnumDataType - component type to be examined
      aUid - uid string
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if invalid

    • isValidOutputTypeUid

      public static <R extends AIiUidEnumDataRecord, O extends AIiUidEnumDataOrigin, GUEDT extends AIiUidEnumDataType<? extends R, O>> boolean isValidOutputTypeUid(@Nonnull GUEDT aEnumDataType, @Nonnull String aUid)
      Type Parameters:
      R - type of the enum uiddata type expected.
      O - origin type
      Parameters:
      aEnumDataType - enum uiddata type to be examined
      aUid - uid string
      Returns:
      true if valid, else false

    • getOrigin

      public static <R extends AIiUidEnumDataRecord, O extends AIiUidEnumDataOrigin, GUEDT extends AIiUidEnumDataType<? extends R, O>> AIiUidEnumDataOrigin getOrigin(@Nonnull GUEDT aEnumDataType, @Nonnull String aUid)
      Type Parameters:
      R - type of the enum uiddata type expected.
      O - origin type
      Parameters:
      aEnumDataType - component type to be examined
      aUid - uid string
      Returns:
      kind class

    • getNamespace

      public static <R extends AIiUidEnumDataRecord, O extends AIiUidEnumDataOrigin, GUEDT extends AIiUidEnumDataType<? extends R, O>> String getNamespace(GUEDT aComponentType, String aUid)
      Type Parameters:
      R - type of the enum uiddata type expected.
      O - origin type
      Parameters:
      aComponentType - component type to be examined
      aUid - uid string
      Returns:
      namespace segment (empty for builtin)

    • splitUidIntoParts

      public static <R extends AIiUidEnumDataRecord, O extends AIiUidEnumDataOrigin, GUEDT extends AIiUidEnumDataType<? extends R, O>> String[] splitUidIntoParts(@Nonnull GUEDT aEnumDataType, @Nonnull String aUid) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Split a UID into parts.
      Type Parameters:
      R - type of the enum uiddata type expected.
      O - origin type
      Parameters:
      aEnumDataType - enum uiddata type to be examined
      aUid - uid string
      Returns:
      parts split according to the definition of the enum uiddata type
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if uid is invalid
